subject: Mathematics
title: Congruences
description: A short lesson to help you apply Congruences.

hook.text: Two numbers can look different yet behave identically under division.

buildup.text: 17 and 5 both leave remainder 5 when divided by 12. We call them congruent mod 12.

discovery.text: Gauss invented this notation in 1801 and it became the language of number theory.

twist.text: ISBN codes and credit card checks use congruences to catch typing errors automatically.

climax.text: Congruences compress infinite sets of numbers into small, manageable groups.

punchline.text: Different numbers, same remainder, same club.

quiz.question: When are two numbers congruent mod 12?
options: They leave the same remainder when divided by 12
